name: Mondoñedo – return via Tronceda river Waterfalls
name_original: Mondoñedo – vuelta por Cascadas del río Tronceda
region_intro: Touring cycling around Mondoñedo offers diverse routes through green and varied landscapes, featuring both rural trails and mountainous terrain. The area's topography includes rolling hills and forests, providing a scenic backdrop for cycling. Routes often combine paved surfaces with unpaved segments, navigating through the region's natural environment. This setting provides a range of challenges for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Mondoñedo The most popular touring cycling route is Mondoñedo – return via Tronceda river Waterfalls, a 15.6 miles (25.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 11 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain and scenic river waterfalls. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Mondoñedo – tour around the Monastery of San Salvador and Trabada, a difficult 29.9 miles (48.1 km) path. This route combines historical sites with scenic Galicia, offering substantial ascent. Local touring cyclists also love the Monastery of San Salvador – Old bridge loop from Abadín, a 61.6 miles (99.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 6 hours 50 minutes. Touring cycling around Mondoñedo is defined by green landscapes, rural trails, and mountainous terrain, with options available for various ability levels. The network includes routes with significant elevation changes and diverse surface types. The routes in Mondoñedo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 700 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Mondoñedo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Mondoñedo?
A: There are over 40 touring cycling routes available around Mondoñedo, offering a diverse range of experiences. Most of these routes are considered difficult, with 31 challenging options and 13 moderate ones, catering to cyclists looking for substantial elevation gains and varied terrain.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Mondoñedo?
A: Mondoñedo's green and varied landscapes are enjoyable throughout much of the year. While specific weather conditions can vary, the region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions outside of the coldest winter months or peak summer heat. It's always advisable to check local weather forecasts before heading out, especially regarding rain impact, as some routes may include unpaved segments.

Q: What are the typical surface types encountered on Mondoñedo's cycling routes?
A: The touring cycling routes around Mondoñedo often feature a combination of paved surfaces and unpaved segments. You can expect to navigate through rural trails and mountainous terrain, providing a varied and engaging experience for cyclists.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Mondoñedo?
A: While many routes in Mondoñedo are rated as difficult or moderate due to significant elevation changes, there are options that can be adapted for beginners. For instance, shorter sections of routes or those with less steep gradients might be suitable. It's recommended to review the elevation profiles and distances of routes to find one that matches your fitness level.

Q: Where can I find challenging touring cycling routes with significant elevation in Mondoñedo?
A: Mondoñedo is known for its challenging routes with substantial elevation gains. For a demanding ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38162460" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of San Salvador – Old bridge loop from Abadín&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 99.1 km with over 1600m of ascent. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017428671/mondonedo-tour-around-the-monastery-of-san-salvador-and-trabada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mondoñedo – tour around the Monastery of San Salvador and Trabada&lt;/a&gt;, a 48.1 km route with over 1400m of climbing.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes that pass by waterfalls in Mondoñedo?
A: Yes, you can find routes that feature beautiful waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017363004/mondonedo-return-via-tronceda-river-waterfalls" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mondoñedo – return via Tronceda river Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; route is a great option, specifically designed to take you past the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5336648" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tronceda River Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5291168" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto do Coro Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is another impressive natural feature in the region.

Q: What natural attractions can I explore while cycling around Mondoñedo?
A: Beyond waterfalls, Mondoñedo offers several natural attractions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5303481" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Consuelo Mine&lt;/a&gt; provides a glimpse into the region's geological past. The wider area also includes the Parque Natural do Xistral, known for its unique ecosystems, and the Cova do Rei Cintolo, Galicia's most extensive cave system, offering a fantastical subterranean landscape.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks accessible by touring bike?
A: Absolutely. Mondoñedo is rich in history. You can cycle to the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/359296" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of San Salvador&lt;/a&gt;, which is featured in routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40318904" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of San Salvador – Beach of the Cathedrals loop from Lourenzá&lt;/a&gt;. The town itself boasts the magnificent &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/368178" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mondoñedo Cathedral&lt;/a&gt; and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6979461" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fonte Vella (Old Fountain)&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes around Mondoñedo?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Mondoñedo are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38162460" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of San Salvador – Old bridge loop from Abadín&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40318904" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of San Salvador – Beach of the Cathedrals loop from Lourenzá&lt;/a&gt;, providing convenient options for exploration.

Q: What are the options for parking near touring cycling routes in Mondoñedo?
A: While specific parking facilities for cyclists are not detailed, Mondoñedo, as a town, generally offers public parking options. For routes starting from specific points, it's advisable to check local maps or komoot route details for suggested starting points which often have nearby parking. Many routes begin and end in or near the town center or other accessible locations.

Q: Are there any cafes or pubs along the cycling routes in Mondoñedo?
A: Mondoñedo and its surrounding villages offer various local establishments where cyclists can stop for refreshments. While specific cafes or pubs directly on every trail are not guaranteed, routes passing through or near populated areas will provide opportunities for a break. Planning your route with komoot allows you to identify towns and villages where you can find amenities.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Mondoñedo?
A: The touring cycling routes in Mondoñedo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 700 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Mondoñedo's varied terrain, often praising the green landscapes, rural trails, and the challenging yet rewarding mountainous sections.
tips: The beautifully finished forecourt of Mondoñedo Cathedral is an excellent place to take a break. Here you can stay in the shade and watch the hustle and bustle of the market. There are enough cafes in the square, which will be happy to provide you with all sorts of goodies.
Natural recreational space with multiple trails for activities along the Tronceda River. Don't forget to see the waterfall and the water channel.
